spans.content: ON KÖTHE DUALS OF ORLICZ-LORENTZ SPACES
ABSTRACT. In this article, we study a number of properties of the Kothe duals
of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces. An explicit description of the order-continuous subspace of
is provided. Moreover, the separability of these spaces are characterized by the growth condition
has the Radon-Nikodym property if and only if the N-function at infinity

spaces is characterized via standard orders between Orlicz functions. As applications of these results, we provide sufficient conditions for M-embedded order-continuous subspaces of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces equipped with the Luxemburg norm and prove the existence of a unique norm-preserving extension on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces equipped with the Orlicz norm.
In this article, we investigate basic properties of the Köthe duals

of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces and their applications to M-embeddedness and the uniqueness of norm-preserving extensions of bounded linear functionals on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces. The Orlicz-Lorentz spaces appeared as a natural generalization of both Orlicz and Lorentz spaces [11]. Since then, some geometrical properties e.g. separability, rotundity or nonsquareness [7,11] of these spaces have been studied. However, comparing to Orlicz spaces, many properties of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces have not been investigated because the explicit description of their Kothe duals was not available until later. Moreover, these Kothe duals exhibit different behaviors from those in the case of Orlicz spaces. It is well-known that the modular functionals for both Orlicz spaces and their Kothe duals have the same structure, so we can study those spaces within the same framework. However, as it turns out, the analogous claim does not hold for Orlicz-Lorentz spaces.
To elaborate this more, in 2013, the first author and Raynaud introduced a new modular
[17] and proved, with Lesnik, that the class of Banach function spaces defined by

requires different approaches from Orlicz-Lorentz spaces. The space
has also been studied in a spirit of abstract Lorentz spaces. For interested readers, we refer to [16].
From the fact that the space

is not an Orlicz-Lorentz space, the properties of this space needs to be studied separately. Such attempts have been done partially in the past. For instance, a sufficient condition for the order-continuity of
has been provided in [8]. In the same paper, it is shown that the space
is a constant function. This consequently shows that Orlicz-Lorentz spaces are never smooth if its complementary
Key words and phrases. Orlicz-Lorentz space, Köthe dual, level function, separability, Banach function space.
-condition. Moreover, the explicit description of the space
allows us to explore geometrical and ideal structures of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces

. It is well-known that the order-continuous subspaces of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces equipped with the Luxemburg norm are M-ideals [13]. Moreover, the Orlicz-Lorentz spaces with the diameter two properties can be characterized by the
-condition [14]. For the diameter two properties, it was essential to compute the fundamental function of the space
in full generality. Since the space
has appeared recently, many properties have not been investigated yet. We attempt to study some of those in the article.

The paper consists of seven parts. In sections 2 and 3 we recall necessary facts on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces and their Kőthe duals. In section 4, from an explicit description of the order-continuous subspace of
(Theorem 4.7), we show that the separability of the space
-condition (Theorem 4.15). This consequently provides a sufficient condition for a class of
to have the Radon-Nikodym property (Theorem 4.16). In section 5, we characterize the inclusion operators between the spaces
defined by different Orlicz functions (Theorem 5.1). In section 6, we identify an interval for the Orlicz norm on

that attains its value (Theorem 6.4). As applications in section 7, we study the M-embeddedness of the order-continuous subspaces of Orlicz-Lorentz spaces (Theorem 7.4) and the uniqueness of norm-preserving extensions of bounded linear functionals on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces equipped with the Orlicz norm (Theorem 7.10, 7.11, and 7.12).

We finish this section with an observation. A Banach space
is said to have the Radon-Nikodym property with respect to

It is well-known that every separable dual space has the RNP, which in turn implies that the space has slices of arbitrarily small diameter (c.f. [6]). This property is in the opposite spectrum of Banach spaces with the Daugavet property and the diameter two properties, which have been active research areas in geometry of Banach spaces. In view of Theorem 4.14, we obtain the following consequence.
-function at infinity that satisfies the appropriate
has the RNP. As a consequence, the unit ball of
has slices of arbitrarily small diameters.

Remark 4.17. For an arbitrary Orlicz function, in particular not being an
-function at infinity, the analogous statement in Theorem 4.16 does not hold. For instance, if
has the Daugavet property, the space does not have the RNP.

7.2. Uniqueness of norm-preserving extension on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces equipped with the Orlicz norm. By the Hahn-Banach extension theorem, any bounded linear functional on a subspace of a Banach space has a norm-preserving extension to the whole space. In this section, we study the uniqueness of such extension from
. In view of [10, Proposition 1.12], we can deduce that every integral functional
has a unique norm-preserving extension to
. Hence, we only consider when the space is equipped with the Orlicz norm.
